steps.title: Use residual signs, not a new regression
steps.content: The line of best fit is already drawn. The question is not asking for its equation; it asks how many actual data points have $y$-values greater than the predicted $y$-values. That means count the dots above the line.

steps.title: Count above the line from left to right
steps.content: Moving left to right prevents double-counting. The points near $x=3.5$, $x=5.9$, $x=6.5$, $x=8.4$, and $x=9.7$ sit above the line. The other four plotted points sit below the line.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: There are $5$ points above the line, so there are $5$ actual $y$-values greater than their predicted values. Choice C is correct.
